goal: Deliver the scatter revert + question-edge fix past the browser/edge cache
done: The HTML referenced js/main.js + css/styles.css (and reading.js/css etc.) with a FIXED ?v=20260619c that was never bumped when the scatter revert + question-board edge fix landed → browsers kept serving the cached old (scatter / unfixed) assets for that unchanged version URL. Bumped ?v=20260619c -> ?v=20260621a across all 32 canonical+www HTML files (ios mirror regenerated by cap sync). Fresh HTML now requests the new asset URLs -> fresh main.js (grid, no scatter) + styles.css (gold-edge question cards). Origin was already correct; this is purely a cache-bust delivery.
verified: origin main.js scatter=0 + styles.css gold-edge=1 (already correct)
notes: LESSON: bump the ?v= in the HTML on EVERY change to a versioned /js or /css asset, else the cached version URL serves stale. Same root cause as the share-page v=g->h poisoning.
